assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md: '## Classification: code change request' and 'Sub-task required: Yes -- this feedback triggers sub-task creation.' The report's Review Comment Classifications table also shows comment 30001 classified as 'code change request' with Sub-task 'Yes'. subtask-30001.md exists and contains the sub-task description.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as a code change request (or upgraded suggestion) and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: '## Classification: code change request' and 'This comment is classified as a **code change request** -- either directly (due to the directive "should" language) or via convention upgrade (if initially classified as a suggestion, the performance-related convention analysis would upgrade it). Either classification path leads to sub-task creation.' Sub-task required: Yes. subtask-30002.md exists.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md: '## Classification: nit' and 'Sub-task required: No.' The report's Review Comment Classifications table shows comment 30003 as 'nit' with Sub-task 'No'. No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md: '## Classification: question' and 'Sub-task required: No.' The report's Review Comment Classifications table shows comment 30004 as 'question' with Sub-task 'No'. No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains sections: '## Repository' (trustify-backend), '## Description', '## Files to Modify', '## Implementation Notes', '## Acceptance Criteria'. subtask-30002.md contains the same sections: '## Repository' (trustify-backend), '## Description', '## Files to Modify', '## Implementation Notes', '## Acceptance Criteria'. Both sub-tasks have all required template sections.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md line 24: '## Target PR' followed by line 25: 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'. subtask-30002.md line 16: '## Target PR' followed by line 17: 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'. Both sub-tasks include the Target PR section with the correct URL.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md lines 27-31: '## Review Context' with Comment ID 30001, File, Reviewer, and the full original comment text starting with 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements...'. subtask-30002.md lines 19-31: '## Review Context' with Comment ID 30002, File, Reviewer, and the full original comment text starting with 'The migration should also add an index on `deleted_at`...'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only: report.md, review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, subtask-30001.md, subtask-30002.md. All output files are classification documents, sub-task descriptions, and the verification report. No source code files (*.rs, *.toml, etc.) appear in the outputs. The report itself does not mention any code modifications being made.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding review classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report's Review Comment Classifications table lists all four comments with their classifications. The report addresses all four comments in its narrative (numbered 1-4) before presenting overall findings.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report's Overall status is 'WARN' with the statement 'Two code change requests from reviewer-a require attention before merge'. The report does not contain any merge commands, auto-merge actions, or merge approvals. The report explicitly states that attention is required before merge, indicating the PR was not auto-merged.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test files added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file) |'. Also lines 116-117: '**Test Change Classification -- ADDITIVE**' and 'Only new test files were added. `tests/api/sbom_delete.rs` is a new file (does not exist on the base branch). No existing test files were modified or deleted.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a full 'Convention Upgrade Eligibility Analysis' subsection with three checks: (1) CONVENTIONS.md check, (2) Codebase pattern check noting 42 SeaORM migrations and established index patterns, (3) Performance-related scrutiny noting extra scrutiny per Style/Conventions sub-agent Check 1c. The report's Style/Conventions section (lines 94-96) also states: 'Comment 30002 (index addition) was evaluated for convention upgrade eligibility. The suggestion to add an index on `deleted_at` aligns with database performance best practices.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path — whether classified directly as code change request based on reviewer language, or upgraded from suggestion via convention analysis
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md explicitly states: 'This comment is classified as a **code change request** -- either directly (due to the directive "should" language) or via convention upgrade (if initially classified as a suggestion, the performance-related convention analysis would upgrade it). Either classification path leads to sub-task creation.' subtask-30002.md exists with the full sub-task description for adding the partial index.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 111-113: '**Eval Quality -- N/A**' followed by 'No eval result reviews found on the PR (3-criteria detection: no `github-actions[bot]` review with `## Eval Results` marker and `sdlc-workflow/run-evals` footer).' This exactly matches the assertion's 3-criteria detection description. The Test Quality row in the report (line 13) shows 'PASS' without any Eval Quality impact.
